items.title: Mortgage Rates and the Lock-In Effect on Housing Supply
items.author: Federal Reserve / FHFA research (working papers, 2023–2024)
items.summary: Finds that homeowners with low pre-2022 mortgages are reluctant to sell, constraining inventory and supporting prices even as demand softens — a key dynamic behind the South Bay's tight supply.

items.title: Seasonality in House Prices and Time-on-Market
items.author: Journal of Housing Economics (survey of findings)
items.summary: Documents that homes listed in spring tend to sell faster and at modest price premiums versus winter, informing optimal listing timing.

items.title: List Price Anchoring and Final Sale Price
items.author: Real Estate Economics (research summary)
items.summary: Shows how the initial list price anchors buyer perception and bidding; strategic pricing below market can generate competition and higher final prices.

items.title: The Effect of Interest Rates on Home Affordability and Demand
items.author: NBER / housing finance working papers
items.summary: Quantifies how each percentage-point change in mortgage rates shifts monthly payments and buyer purchasing power, highly relevant to high-price South Bay markets.

items.title: iBuyers, Agents, and Seller Outcomes
items.author: Journal of Real Estate research (2020–2022)
items.summary: Compares net proceeds and speed across selling channels, generally finding traditional agent-assisted sales yield higher net prices despite convenience alternatives.
